qa_pairs.question: What is the primary objective of the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure renewal project by the Digital Research Alliance of Canada?
qa_pairs.answer: The primary objective is to improve High Performance Computing (HPC) and Cloud services for Canadian researchers by replacing nearly 80% of end-of-life equipment.

qa_pairs.question: When did the renewal of the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure begin and when were the new systems made available to users?
qa_pairs.answer: The infrastructure renewal began in winter 2024–2025, and new systems were made available to users throughout 2025 and early 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What are the main benefits provided by the new Advanced Research Computing systems?
qa_pairs.answer: The new systems provide faster processing speeds, greater storage capacity, and improved reliability.

qa_pairs.question: Which legacy Advanced Research Computing systems have been replaced by new ones, according to the system details?
qa_pairs.answer: Béluga was replaced by Rorqual, Cedar by Fir, Niagara and Mist by Trillium, and Graham by Nibi. The Arbutus Cloud was upgraded but retains its name.

qa_pairs.question: What actions are required for users with 2025–2026 allocations on the legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Both RAC recipients and RAS users with 2025–2026 allocations on legacy Arbutus are responsible for migrating existing resources before August 31, 2026, as soon as access to the new Arbutus Cloud is available.

qa_pairs.question: How does authentication differ for the new Arbutus Cloud system?
qa_pairs.answer: The new Arbutus Cloud introduces single sign-on (SSO) authentication with multi-factor authentication (MFA), requiring users to select “Authenticate using Digital Research Alliance of Canada” when logging in.

qa_pairs.question: What is the decommissioning status of the Béluga compute service and when will its data access end?
qa_pairs.answer: The Béluga compute service was stopped with the deployment of Rorqual and will not return. Access to all remaining data on Béluga will end on June 20, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Are files stored on the retired Cedar and Graham compute clusters still accessible?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, files stored on Cedar are available on Fir, and files on Graham are available on Nibi, as these clusters share the same file systems, so no action is required for stored files.

qa_pairs.question: How does the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure transition affect Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) awards?
qa_pairs.answer: The 2025/26 RAC allocations will only become available when all new clusters are in service, and users may experience a period where neither 2024 nor 2025 awards are available, though default allocations will be usable.

qa_pairs.question: What is recommended for researchers to consider when planning programs due to the installation and transition to the new Advanced Research Computing infrastructure?
qa_pairs.answer: Researchers are recommended to consider the possibility of unavoidable outages and reductions when planning research programs or graduate examinations.

qa_pairs.question: Who is responsible for migrating user data to the new Advanced Research Computing systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Data migration to the new systems is the responsibility of each National Host Site, which will inform users of the necessary steps.

qa_pairs.question: Will user files be deleted during a complete data centre shutdown as part of the Advanced Research Computing renewal activities?
qa_pairs.answer: No, user files will not be deleted; National Host Sites will migrate /project and /home data to new storage systems, and tape and /nearline data will remain unchanged.

qa_pairs.question: Will existing jobs and applications run on the new Advanced Research Computing systems, and will software still be available?
qa_pairs.answer: Generally yes, but new CPUs and GPUs may require recompilation or reconfiguration. The standard software environment and commercial licensed software are planned to be available.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find training resources and opportunities related to the new Alliance clusters and systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can discover training opportunities through Explora and find specific courses like "Intro to SciNet and Trillium" or "Mastering GPU Efficiency" on the provided links.

qa_pairs.question: Whom should users contact for questions regarding the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure transition or for technical support?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should contact the technical support team for any questions regarding the transition.
